description: Make organisation/contact hard-delete safe. DELETE /api/organisations/{id} must null organisation_id on all child contacts and pitches before deleting the org. DELETE /api/contacts/{id} must remove the contact's PitchContact and MeetingAttendee join rows in the same transaction. Keep delete admin-only and create admin+assessor.
expectedBehavior: Deleting an organisation returns 200 and leaves linked contacts and pitches in place with organisation_id = null
Deleting a contact returns 200 and removes its PitchContact and MeetingAttendee rows; parent pitches and meetings remain retrievable
Deleting an unknown organisation or contact id returns 404
assessor and viewer get 403 on delete; create stays 200 for admin/assessor and 403 for viewer
No dangling foreign keys remain after either delete
verificationSteps: cd backend && pytest tests/test_organisations.py -k 'delete or orphan or rbac'
cd backend && pytest tests/test_contacts.py -k 'delete or cascade or rbac'

description: Add 'Add' and per-row 'Remove' controls to OrganisationsPage and ContactsPage. Add opens a create form (modal/inline) and posts to the API, inserting the new row on success. Remove requires a confirmation step, then deletes and removes the row. Role-gate the controls: Add for admin+assessor, Remove for admin only; viewers see a read-only table. Surface API errors without mutating the table.
expectedBehavior: Admin sees Add and per-row Remove; assessor sees Add only; viewer sees neither
Submitting the Add form calls api.post and renders the new row on resolve
Remove shows a confirmation; confirming calls api.delete and removes the row; cancelling does not call the API
A rejected delete leaves the row present and shows an error

description: Confirm and pin PATCH /api/pitches/{id} semantics. Partial update of allowed fields only; admin+assessor allowed, viewer 403, unknown id 404. Enforce the audit invariant that current_stage is NOT settable via PATCH (it is absent from PitchUpdate) so no stage change can occur without a PitchStageHistory row.
expectedBehavior: admin/assessor PATCH updates only supplied fields and returns 200; omitted fields preserved
viewer PATCH -> 403; unauthenticated -> 401; unknown id -> 404
A PATCH body containing current_stage returns 200 but leaves the stage unchanged and writes no stage-history row
verificationSteps: cd backend && pytest tests/test_pitches.py -k 'patch or update or stage_immutable'

description: Add an 'Edit' button on PitchDetailPage (admin/assessor only) that navigates to a new /pitches/:id/edit route. The edit route reuses the create form, pre-filled from the pitch, and submits PATCH on save then returns to the detail page; Cancel returns without calling the API. A viewer hitting the edit route is redirected. Keep the 'Confidential' badge as a visual marker only (no visibility change).
expectedBehavior: Detail page shows Edit for admin/assessor and hides it for viewer
Edit route fetches the pitch and renders the form pre-filled
Saving calls api.patch with changed fields then navigates to the detail route; Cancel makes no api.patch call
Viewer navigating to the edit route is redirected (no form rendered)
Confidential pitches render a badge and remain openable by all roles

description: Confirm and pin POST /api/pitches/{id}/stage. Updates current_stage and appends a PitchStageHistory row (from/to/changed_by/note). admin+assessor allowed; viewer 403; invalid stage 422; unknown id 404. Same endpoint backs both drag and right-click.
expectedBehavior: admin/assessor stage change returns 200 with current_stage updated
A history row is appended capturing from_stage, to_stage and the acting user as changed_by_id
viewer -> 403; unauthenticated -> 401; invalid new_stage -> 422; unknown id -> 404

description: Add a right-click (onContextMenu) stage picker to PitchCard, listing PIPELINE_STAGES with the current stage marked non-selectable. Selecting a stage uses the board's existing optimistic-move + stage-API call, reverting the card on failure (same path as drag). Hide the menu for viewers. Close on selection, outside click, and Escape.
expectedBehavior: Right-click opens a menu listing all stages with the current one marked and not selectable
Selecting a new stage moves the card and calls api.post('/pitches/{id}/stage', ...)
On a rejected call the card reverts to its original column
Menu closes on outside click and on Escape
Viewers get no app stage menu on right-click

description: Close the user-management info-disclosure hole without breaking name resolution. Restrict GET /api/users and GET /api/users/{id} to admin only (full UserOut). Add GET /api/users/directory available to any authenticated user, returning a minimal {id, display_name} shape via a dedicated UserDirectoryOut schema (no email/role/is_active/azure_oid). Declare /directory and /me before /{id}. Keep POST/PATCH admin-only.
expectedBehavior: GET /api/users and /{id}: 200 for admin, 403 for assessor/viewer, 401 unauthenticated
GET /api/users/directory: 200 for admin, assessor and viewer; 401 unauthenticated
Directory entries contain exactly id and display_name and never email, role, is_active or azure_oid
GET /api/users/me stays available to any authenticated user
POST/PATCH users remain admin-only (assessor/viewer -> 403)

description: Guard /admin/users with an admin-only route wrapper that redirects non-admins (UsersPage never renders for them); keep the Sidebar 'Users' link admin-only. Repoint the six name-resolution callers (PipelinePage, PitchCreatePage, PitchDetailPage, AssessmentsPage, AssessmentDetailPage, MeetingAttendees) from GET /api/users to GET /api/users/directory so assessor/viewer flows keep working.
expectedBehavior: Sidebar shows the Users link for admin and hides it for assessor/viewer
Rendering /admin/users as assessor or viewer redirects away without rendering UsersPage; admin renders it
Name-resolution surfaces call /api/users/directory (not /api/users) and render for an assessor
